National accounts chain-volume — latest unpublished quarter

Backcast for March 2026 — ABS BoP not yet published

ABS Balance of Payments chain-volume measures lag actual quarter-end by ~2 months. These estimates anticipate the upcoming release by applying the validated growth-rate pass-through bridge: YoY growth in DISR physical tonnes (back-cast from complete PortWatch data) is chained onto the most recent ABS chain-volume level.

Iron ore
45,542A$m
80% CI · 43,604 – 47,566 A$m+0.5% YoY
Coal (total)
22,363A$m
80% CI · 21,446 – 23,318 A$m+1.4% YoY

Why the nowcast says what it says

Each headline above broken into additive contributions vs the seasonal-naive baseline (same quarter last year). All values in Mt; rows sum to the nowcast up to a small non-linearity in exp(Δlog) − 1. “Model anchor adj.” combines the intercept and the lag-4 coefficient drift — effectively zero for bojo specs (which impose β4 = 1), small for the others. “Other” fires only when a price-augmented spec wins production for a commodity.

commodity last year (V_{Q-4}) tonnage signal model anchor adj. other = nowcast
Iron ore 238.7 Mt -4.9 +2.2 +0.0 236.0 Mt
Metallurgical coal 37.0 Mt +1.6 -0.4 +0.0 38.2 Mt
Thermal coal 47.8 Mt +1.4 +0.4 +0.0 49.6 Mt

Forecast vs actual — closed quarters

No closed quarters with a prior in-window nowcast yet. Comparisons appear here once a quarter we nowcasted within 42 days of close has been published by DISR.

Band calibration

Empirical band coverage on backtest quarters, reconstructed at share_observed = 0 (full residual bootstrap, no in-quarter shrink). Well-calibrated bands produce values near 0.80 and 0.95. Combination specs (equal_avg, inv_mse) lack a single lm fit and are omitted.
★ commodity spec n (OOS) coverage 80% (target 0.80) coverage 95% (target 0.95)
iron_ore aggregate 7 0.57 0.86
★ iron_ore bojo 7 0.71 0.86
iron_ore lagged 7 0.71 0.71
iron_ore midas 7 0.71 0.71
iron_ore price_aug 7 0.57 0.71
coal_met aggregate 7 0.57 0.86
coal_met bojo 7 0.71 0.71
coal_met lagged 7 0.57 0.86
★ coal_met midas 7 0.86 1.00
coal_met price_aug 7 0.57 0.71
coal_thermal aggregate 7 0.57 0.71

coal_thermal lagged 7 0.29 0.71
coal_thermal midas 7 0.43 0.86
coal_thermal price_aug 7 0.57 0.71

Bridge diagnostics

★ marks the production-deployed spec per commodity (best of the candidate set + combinations by backtest RMSE). R² is in-sample, R²_oos is walk-forward out-of-sample. p(β_lag4=1) is the Wald-test p-value for the YoY-on-YoY restriction (NA for bojo, which imposes it; equal_avg and inv_mse are combination forecasts and have no in-sample β). p(Chow) tests for a structural break at the midpoint of the training sample; values < 0.05 suggest the AIS↔︎DISR relationship shifted within the fit window. RMSE/naive < 1 means the spec beats the seasonal random walk.
★ commodity spec R² R²_oos β_T (se) β_lag4 p(β_lag4=1) p(Chow) rmse_valid RMSE/naive

Flagged anomalies

Largest |z|-score departures from the seasonal daily norm (±7-day window) in the last 28 days. Seasonal norm is built from training-window observations only; z > 0 is unusually busy, z < 0 unusually quiet.
date port commodity tonnage (kt) expected (kt) z

Methodology

Production equation per commodity

These are the actual equations driving the headline nowcast above. One per commodity, picked by walk-forward backtest RMSE from a bench of {aggregate, midas, bojo, lagged, price_aug, demand_aug, equal_avg, inv_mse} specs.

Iron ore — bojo spec

\[\Delta_{4}\log V_{Q} \;=\; 0.009 \;+ 0.504 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q} \;+\; \varepsilon_{Q}\]

Wald test of \(\beta_{lag4}=1\): aggregate p = 0.36, midas p = 0.70. Restriction not rejected; pure YoY-on-YoY wins the OOS leaderboard at RMSE = 4.51 Mt (0.78× naive).

Metallurgical coal — midas spec

\[\log V_{Q} \;=\; 0.940 \;- 0.011 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q,m1} \;+ 0.252 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q,m2} \;- 0.019 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q,m3} \;+ 0.737 \,\log V_{Q-4} \;+\; \varepsilon_{Q}\]

Wald test of \(\beta_{lag4}=1\): aggregate p = 0.06, midas p = 0.00. OOS RMSE = 1.10 Mt (0.49× naive); within-quarter monthly timing carries enough signal to justify the extra parameters.

Thermal coal — bojo spec

\[\Delta_{4}\log V_{Q} \;=\; 0.009 \;+ 1.340 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q} \;+\; \varepsilon_{Q}\]

Wald test of \(\beta_{lag4}=1\): aggregate p = 0.00, midas p = 0.01. Restriction not rejected; pure YoY-on-YoY wins the OOS leaderboard at RMSE = 2.21 Mt (0.89× naive).

Conventions

  • \(V_{Q}\) is DISR Resources & Energy Quarterly Table 16 physical tonnage (Mt) for the commodity. Coal is metallurgical + thermal summed.
  • \(T_{Q}\) is PortWatch AIS daily tonnage summed across the commodity’s ports over quarter \(Q\). The current-quarter partial-month scale-up anchors on the data’s latest observation date (not Sys.Date()), so PortWatch’s 7–14 day refresh lag doesn’t bias the estimate down.
  • \(\Delta_{4}\) is the year-on-year log difference. The YoY transform strips deterministic seasonality, so no explicit STL / X-13 step is needed at our sample size (n ≈ 23 quarters).
  • Newey-West HAC standard errors with lag = 1. Uncertainty bands from residual bootstrap, scaled by \(\sqrt{1 - \text{share\_observed}}\) so they collapse as the current quarter fills in.
  • Full derivation, candidate bench, and the literature this draws on: docs/METHODOLOGY.md.
